diagrams.title: Sequential Access Method
diagrams.description: Shows how records are accessed one after another from beginning to end
diagrams.purpose: Understand that to reach record N, all preceding records must be read. Ideal for magnetic tape and batch processing.

diagrams.title: Direct Access Method with Relative Record Numbers
diagrams.description: Illustrates how each record has a direct address calculated from RRN
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate O(1) access time calculation and direct record retrieval without searching.

diagrams.title: Dense vs Sparse Index Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows structure differences between dense and sparse indexes
elements: Sparse: Index Entry → Data Block (containing multiple records)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why dense index needs more storage but provides faster individual record access.

diagrams.title: ISAM File Organization
diagrams.description: Shows multi-level index structure combining sequential data with hierarchical indexes
elements: Data flow: Index → Track → Data Record
diagrams.purpose: Understand how ISAM provides both fast random access and efficient sequential processing.

diagrams.title: B+ Tree Index Structure
diagrams.description: Demonstrates balanced tree structure used for file indexes
elements: All leaf nodes linked for sequential access
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why B+ trees provide O(log n) access and efficient range queries.

diagrams.title: Hash-based File Access
diagrams.description: Shows hash function mapping keys to bucket addresses with collision handling
diagrams.purpose: Understand how hashing achieves O(1) average access and how collisions are managed.
